description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer who must prove—by “policy-as-code”—that only pre-approved Python packages are present in a new virtual environment.  
    The security team has already supplied an allow-list file located at:

        /home/user/devsecops-policy/allowed_packages.txt

    Its exact, one-line content is:

        requests==2.31.0

    Your job is to:

    1. Create a Python virtual environment inside the project directory at  
       /home/user/devsecops-policy/venv

    2. Install every package pinned in the allow-list (there is only one: requests==2.31.0).  
       No other packages may be installed.

    3. Generate a single CSV compliance report at  
       /home/user/devsecops-policy/logs/compliance_report.csv  
       (create the logs directory if it does not exist).

       The report must have exactly two rows and four comma-separated columns with no extra
       whitespace, no additional rows, and a terminating newline character:

            PACKAGE,INSTALLED_VERSION,ALLOWED_VERSION,STATUS
            requests,2.31.0,2.31.0,COMPLIANT

       • Row-1 is the fixed header shown above (all caps).  
       • Row-2 must list the installed package, the version detected inside the virtual
         environment, the version declared in allowed_packages.txt, and the word
         “COMPLIANT”.  
       • Cells are separated by the literal comma character “,”.  
       • Do not include quotes around values.  
       • The order of columns and rows must exactly match the example.

    4. Do not modify the supplied allow-list file.

    When you are finished, the automated test will confirm:
       • The virtual-environment directory /home/user/devsecops-policy/venv exists.  
       • Activating that venv and running “pip show requests” returns version 2.31.0.  
       • The file /home/user/devsecops-policy/logs/compliance_report.csv exists and its
         content matches the required 2-row, 4-column CSV shown above.

    Hint: In most shells only 2-3 commands are needed: create venv, install package from the
    allow-list, then create the CSV.
truth:
Pre-existing file before task starts:
      Path: /home/user/devsecops-policy/allowed_packages.txt
      Contents (with trailing newline): 
        requests==2.31.0\n

    Verification file the agent must create:
      Path: /home/user/devsecops-policy/logs/compliance_report.csv
      Required exact contents (with trailing newline):
        PACKAGE,INSTALLED_VERSION,ALLOWED_VERSION,STATUS\n
        requests,2.31.0,2.31.0,COMPLIANT\n
